stemHtml: The pilot of an aircraft reports 'Final Runway 28' and the tower controller replies 'Continue approach, wind 180 degrees, 15 knots'. How should the pilot interpret this report?

options.a: The aircraft can continue the approach but the Controller strongly recommends the crew to make an early decision to go around.
options.b: The runway is NOT clear yet but expected to be clear in due time. The Controller delays the landing clearance until the runway is clear.
options.c: The runway is NOT clear yet but expected to be clear in due time. The aircraft is allowed to land WITHOUT further instructions as soon as the runway is clear.
options.d: The aircraft is allowed to land under its own responsibility. A clearance is NOT issued as the Controller CANNOT take responsibility in the current wind conditions.
